model_narrative: We schedule electricity generation across the demand blocks of a day using several generator types. For each generator type in each demand block we decide how many units to commit, how much power they produce in total, and how many units are newly started up. Each committed unit carries a fixed hourly operating cost, output above the minimum level carries an incremental cost, and bringing a unit online carries a startup cost. The objective is to meet the schedule at the lowest total operating cost over the whole day.

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the demand blocks of the day in chronological order; the first member is the opening block and each later member immediately follows the one before it

sets.name: g
sets.doc: the generator types available for commitment

params.name: dem
params.doc: the power demand that must be met in each demand block, in 1000 MW

params.name: dur
params.doc: the length of each demand block, in hours

params.name: minpow
params.doc: the minimum output that one committed unit of a generator type must produce when it is running, in 1000 MW per unit

params.name: maxpow
params.doc: the maximum output that one committed unit of a generator type can produce, in 1000 MW per unit

params.name: costmin
params.doc: the fixed operating cost charged per hour for each committed unit running at its minimum output

params.name: costinc
params.doc: the incremental cost of output produced above the minimum level

params.name: start
params.doc: the startup cost charged each time a unit of a generator type is brought online

params.name: number
params.doc: the number of available units of each generator type, in whole units

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the total power output of a generator type in a demand block, in 1000 MW

vars.name: n
vars.doc: the number of committed units of a generator type running in a demand block, a whole number

vars.name: s
vars.doc: the number of units of a generator type started up at the beginning of a demand block

constraints.name: x[g,
constraints.intent: In every demand block the power produced has to exactly meet what is needed. For each demand block, the total output of all generator types together must equal the power demand for that block.

constraints.name: maxpow[g]
constraints.intent: Each demand block has to carry a spinning reserve cushion above the demand. For each demand block, the combined maximum output that all committed units could produce must be at least fifteen percent more than the power demand for that block.

constraints.name: t.data())
constraints.intent: Startups have to be accounted for whenever the fleet of a generator type grows from one block to the next. For each generator type and each demand block other than the opening one, the number of units started up at the beginning of that block must be at least the increase in committed units from the immediately preceding block to that block. The opening block has no preceding block, so this is not imposed there.

constraints.name: x[g,
constraints.intent: A running generator type cannot be pushed below its floor. For each generator type and each demand block, the total output must be at least the per-unit minimum output times the number of committed units.

constraints.name: x[g,
constraints.intent: A generator type cannot be pushed above what its committed units can deliver. For each generator type and each demand block, the total output must not exceed the per-unit maximum output times the number of committed units.

constraints.name: n[g,
constraints.intent: Only as many units as actually exist can be committed. For each generator type and each demand block, the number of committed units must not exceed the number of available units of that type.

query: Grid operators want the big type-3 fleet pulling more total energy across the day for firmer coverage. There's a proposal on the table to have type-3 deliver at least 8 (1000 MW) of combined output summed over all the demand blocks. What does the optimizer return for the day's total cost?
expected_pyomo: model.t3_out_floor = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x['type-3', t] for t in model.t) >= 8)

query: There's a proposal to lean less on the type-2 fleet overall — cap the total type-2 commitments across the day at 5 unit-blocks. What does that do to cost?
expected_pyomo: model.t2_total = Constraint(expr=sum(model.n['type-2', t] for t in model.t) <= 5)

query: Engineering is pitching an always-on reserve posture for the big units — keep at least one type-3 generator committed in every demand block, around the clock, so there's always large-unit headroom available. Plug that in and walk me through the resulting cost.

query: We're evaluating a tighter overnight footprint — hold total units online across all types in the 12pm-6am block to 12. What does the optimizer return for cost?
expected_pyomo: model.overnight_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.n[g, '12pm-6am'] for g in model.g) <= 12)

query: We lean hard on the small type-1 units in the afternoon peak while the more efficient type-2 fleet sits underused. Shouldn't the plan commit at least as many type-2 units as type-1 units during the 3pm-6pm block, so the mid-size fleet carries its fair share? What's keeping the model from making that move?

query: Type-1 is maxed out in every single block — it's clearly carrying the whole schedule. Wouldn't it be healthier to hold type-1 to no more than 10 committed units in each demand block and let the larger units pick up the slack? What's the model seeing that I'm not?

query: Type-1 seems to be carrying the whole day. Is there a reason we couldn't hold its total commitments across the day to 55 unit-blocks and let the others share more load? What's the tradeoff?
expected_pyomo: model.t1_total_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.n['type-1', t] for t in model.t) <= 55)

query: Two things bug me about the peak-and-overnight pattern: type-1 runs flat out in the afternoon peak, and meanwhile we barely keep any type-2 units online through the night. Couldn't we pair a tighter peak posture with a stronger overnight base — hold type-1 to at most 8 committed units in the 3pm-6pm block AND keep at least 6 type-2 units online in the 12pm-6am block? Walk me through what's pulling the plan the other way.
